pr.title: Get un_8031D288_OnEnter matched up to regswaps and load
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Matched the vi0401 scene enter routine un_8031D288_OnEnter close enough to be limited to register swaps and a load difference. The placeholder was replaced with a full C implementation that initializes audio/effects, loads Vi0401/IfAll scene symbols and character animation data, creates fog/light/camera/model GObjs, wires GX callbacks/procs, starts scene animations, spawns the character display, and plays three audio cues. The header signature was tightened from UNK_T to void*. No review comments were included in the provided slice.
what_changed: Replaced the non-decompiled marker `/// #un_8031D288_OnEnter` in `src/melee/vi/vi0401.c` with a concrete implementation.
Added includes needed by the new implementation: `ef/efasync.h`, `ef/eflib.h`, `lb/lbarchive.h`, `sc/types.h`, and baselib fog/gxlink/object/proc/lobj headers.
Changed `un_804D6F48`, `un_804D6F4C`, and `un_804D6F50` into static `SceneDesc*` globals used for `visual0401Scene`, `visual0401InfoScene`, and `ScInfDmg_scene_data`.
Changed prior external declarations for `un_804D6F54` and `un_804D6F58` into static globals in `vi0401.c`.
Implemented setup for audio/effect state with `lbAudioAx_800236DC`, `efLib_8005B4B8`, and `efAsync_8006737C(0)`.
Loaded scene and info assets with `lbArchive_LoadSymbols`, including `Vi0401.dat`, `IfAll.dat`, and a character animation archive from `viGetCharAnimByIndex(char_index)`.
Created fog, light, camera, and model GObjs using HSD/baselib creation, object binding, GX link setup, animation request, animation evaluation, and process registration calls.
Called `un_8031D030` using the first two bytes of the incoming `data` pointer as character index and costume.
Called `vi_8031C9B4(1, 0)` and played three sounds with `lbAudioAx_800237A8` IDs `0x61a88`, `0x61a89`, and `0x61a8a`.
smart_moves: The implementation preserves the likely binary-sensitive ordering of setup calls: audio/effects first, archive loads next, then fog/light/camera/model construction, then character setup and sound playback.

Using `SceneDesc*` for the loaded scene symbols exposes the structure behind `visual0401Scene`, `visual0401InfoScene`, and `ScInfDmg_scene_data`, enabling direct typed access to cameras, fogs, lights, and models.
The header update from `UNK_T` to `void*` is minimal but useful: the function still treats the argument as raw scene-enter data while documenting pointer semantics more accurately.
The code uses existing HSD helper APIs (`HSD_FogLoadDesc`, `lb_80013B14`, `HSD_JObjLoadJoint`, `gm_8016895C`, animation helpers, and GX callbacks) rather than open-coding scene setup.
The PR title explicitly states the remaining mismatch quality: matched up to register swaps and a load, which is valuable context for future cleanup.
decomp_lessons: For scene-enter routines, matching often requires reconstructing a strict asset-loading and GObj-creation sequence rather than only identifying the high-level scene behavior.

Archive-loaded scene symbols should be typed to existing scene descriptor structures when the diff evidence supports it; this can reduce `UNK_T` usage and make loops over cameras/fogs/lights/models straightforward.
When an entrypoint consumes opaque menu or scene data, a `void*` parameter plus explicit byte extraction can be preferable to leaving the public declaration as `UNK_T`.
PR titles that mention residual mismatch causes such as register swaps or load differences are useful matching evidence and should be preserved in future notes.
Static file-scope globals can replace extern declarations when the evidence shows the data is owned and used locally within the translation unit.
naming_conventions: `un_8031D288_OnEnter` follows the project convention of address-based unknown scene lifecycle names with an `_OnEnter` suffix.
`un_8031D698_OnFrame` in the same header uses the matching `_OnFrame` lifecycle suffix.
Unknown file-scope globals retain address-derived names such as `un_804D6F48`, `un_804D6F4C`, `un_804D6F50`, `un_804D6F54`, and `un_804D6F58` until better semantic names are justified.
Internal helper names such as `fn_8031D168` remain address-derived when no semantic name is evidenced.
Scene asset symbol names remain literal and case-sensitive: `visual0401Scene`, `visual0401InfoScene`, and `ScInfDmg_scene_data`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use typed locals for HSD objects (`HSD_CObj*`, `HSD_GObj*`, `HSD_Fog*`, `HSD_LObj*`, `HSD_JObj*`) to guide codegen for object creation and callback setup.
Preserve loop structure over null-terminated `SceneDesc->models` arrays for model GObj creation.
Extract `char_index` as `u8 char_index = ((u8*) data)[0]` before archive loading, then reuse `((u8*) data)[0]` and `[1]` for the later character/costume setup call.
Cast camera descriptors to `HSD_CameraDescPerspective*` when calling `lb_80013B14`.
The stated remaining issues are register swaps and one load difference; no exact asm hunk or reviewer explanation was provided in the slice.

A suspicious-looking assignment in the excerpt, `gobj->gxlink_prios = 0x801` after creating the second camera object, should be treated as evidence from the diff rather than corrected without further matching data.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The provided context reports one issue comment/text record, but its body was not included, so no reviewer intent or feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match `un_8031D288_OnEnter` beyond register swaps and the load difference.
Search for uses or renames of `un_804D6F48`, `un_804D6F4C`, `un_804D6F50`, `un_804D6F54`, and `un_804D6F58` to see whether better semantic names were introduced.
Compare other `vi` scene enter routines for reusable patterns in fog/light/camera/model GObj setup.
Check whether the `gobj->gxlink_prios = 0x801` assignment after second camera creation was later changed to `cam_gobj->gxlink_prios` or intentionally preserved for matching.
